Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Airtable relocation records for all hazmat-related moves using airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Relocations") to retrieve all records, or airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Special Requirements", value: "hazmat" or "DOT" or "Class 9" or "lithium" or "laser" or similar). Discovery: 3 relocation records contain explicit DOT/hazmat keywords in Special Requirements Mei-Lin Zhao / GreenStack (Houston→Portland, Class 9 lithium-ion, recMeiLinZhaoHoustonPortland), Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic (Columbus→Seattle, Class 3B laser cutter, recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle), Raj Patel / NorthWind (Chicago→Denver, lithium UPS servers Class 9, recRajPatelChicagoDenver). A 4th record Joaquín Reyes / GreenStack (Phoenix→Seattle, 900-lb specialty rig non-hazmat, recJoaquinReyesPhoenixSeattle) does not contain hazmat/DOT keywords in Airtable but is identified as DOT-relevant via the Linear compliance project (OE 2), where it was flagged for overweight/oversize permit and included in the DOT response for transparency.
OE 2: Search Linear for the DOT compliance project and associated tickets using linear_list_projects (query: "DOT" or "compliance" or similar) and/or linear_list_issues (project: project_dot_compliance_audit). Discovery: project "DOT Compliance Audit Q1/Q2 2026 Hazmat Shipments" (project_dot_compliance_audit), led by Chloe Vance, state: started. Contains 5 issues: issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack (state_done, all docs verified), issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic (state_in_progress, placarding certificate missing), issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind (state_done but comment reveals chain-of-custody log is actually missing), issue_dot_docs_reyes_greenstack (state_done, non-hazmat oversize docs verified), issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert (state_todo, urgent contact Greg Pallone at Swift for signed placarding cert).
OE 3: Read comments on issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind using linear_list_comments (issueId: issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind). Discovery: comment linear_comment_093d903c5cb9 flags that the ticket is marked Done but no signed custody log can be found in the move packet, vendor email trail, or incident materials. Raj Patel's Airtable record still shows Status = In Progress. Documentation state is split across systems the Raj Patel package is NOT actually complete despite the ticket being closed.
OE 4: Read comments across DOT compliance issues using linear_list_comments, including on issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack where comment_chloe_swift_confirmation is cross-filed. Discovery: Greg Pallone at Swift confirmed (April 22) that DOT hazmat placarding was completed for Keiko Tanaka's shipment driver Mike Becerra affixed placards at Columbus pickup April 15 per 49 CFR 172.504, signed at loading and countersigned at Swift Seattle. Swift job #SR-2026-04-1538. Greg is sending scanned PDFs; awaiting signed originals.
OE 5: Search emails for the PHMSA/DOT compliance inquiry using search_emails (query: "PHMSA" or "DOT compliance" or "Linda Castellano" or similar). Discovery: original PHMSA letter (Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187, deadline May 21), Marcus Thorne's response identifying 4 shipments and requesting 15-day extension, DOT extension letter granting new deadline June 5 requiring signed originals or certified copies, and Marcus's internal forward to Chloe flagging that scanned PDFs may not suffice.
OE 6: Search emails for Greg Pallone's confirmation about the Keiko Tanaka placarding certificate using search_emails (query: "Greg Pallone" or "Swift placarding" or similar). Discovery: Greg confirmed placarding was completed and is sending scanned PDFs of the signed placarding certificate, Mosaic laser liability waiver, and Swift's carrier certification. Per DOT's extension letter, signed originals or certified copies are required scans alone may not suffice.
OE 7: Search Slack #operations (C006) for hazmat/compliance discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "hazmat" or "DOT" or "Raj Patel chain" or similar) or conversations_history (channel_id: C006). Discovery: Chloe's reconstructed timeline of the Raj Patel chain-of-custody failure (SentinelChain crew loaded at 7:00 AM before the 9:00 AM escort, overnight storage at South San Francisco warehouse violated all three of Victor Huang's non-negotiables), a follow-up flagging the ticket as Done with no custody log and Airtable still showing In Progress, and Blessing Okafor's field note about Swift's driver spending 45 minutes on the DOT packet for Emilia Cruz's household move.
OE 8: Look up coordinator and recipient contact information using contacts_search_contacts or contacts_get_contacts for Javier Morales (javier.morales@moveops.com), Blessing Okafor (blessing.okafor@moveops.com), Fatimah Al-Rashidi (fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com), and Marcus Thorne (marcus.thorne@moveops.com).
OE 9: Create a Linear ticket for the Raj Patel / NorthWind chain-of-custody documentation gap using linear_create_issue (team: team_operations, project: project_dot_compliance_audit). Title related to "Raj Patel chain-of-custody gap" or similar, describing that the ticket was closed prematurely with no signed custody log on file, priority urgent/P1 (DOT deadline June 5), assignee Blessing Okafor or Chloe Vance.
OE 10: Create a Linear ticket or update the existing issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert (or add a comment) noting that DOT requires signed originals or certified copies per the extension letter scanned PDFs from Swift alone will not suffice for the Keiko Tanaka placarding certificate.
OE 11: Contact coordinators responsible for moves with documentation gaps via send_email or conversations_add_message Blessing Okafor (blessing.okafor@moveops.com) regarding the Raj Patel missing chain-of-custody log, and Javier Morales (javier.morales@moveops.com) regarding the Keiko Tanaka placarding certificate (need signed originals from Swift, not just scans). Both messages should reference the June 5 DOT deadline.
OE 12: Update Airtable relocation records with DOT compliance status by appending notes to the "Special Requirements" field (multilineText) using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01) recMeiLinZhaoHoustonPortland (DOT docs complete), recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle (DOT docs pending awaiting signed placarding cert from Swift), recRajPatelChicagoDenver (DOT docs gap missing signed chain-of-custody log), recJoaquinReyesPhoenixSeattle (DOT docs complete non-hazmat, oversize permit verified).
OE 13: Send email to Marcus Thorne (marcus.thorne@moveops.com) with the full compliance package summary using send_email (from: chloe.vance@moveops.com). Email should cover all 4 shipments and their status, the two gaps (Tanaka placarding cert, Patel custody log), the June 5 deadline (Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187), that DOT requires signed originals, actions taken (tickets, coordinators contacted, Airtable updated), and the Raj Patel chain-of-custody failure context.
OE 14: Post a summary in Slack #operations (channel_id: C006) using conversations_add_message covering the 4 shipments' compliance status, what is complete (Zhao, Reyes), what needs action (Tanaka Javier to get signed originals from Swift; Patel Blessing to address missing custody log), the June 5 deadline, and that all documentation must be signed originals or certified copies.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 1: Search Airtable relocation records for all hazmat-related moves using airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Relocations") to retrieve all records, or airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Special Requirements", value: "hazmat" or "DOT" or "Class 9" or "lithium" or "laser" or similar). A 4th record Joaquín Reyes / GreenStack (Phoenix→Seattle, 900-lb specialty rig non-hazmat, recJoaquinReyesPhoenixSeattle) does not contain hazmat/DOT keywords in Airtable but is identified as DOT-relevant via the Linear compliance project (OE 2), where it was flagged for overweight/oversize permit and included in the DOT response for transparency.

OE 3: Read comments on issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind using linear_list_comments (issueId: issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind). Raj Patel's Airtable record still shows Status = In Progress. Documentation state is split across systems the Raj Patel package is NOT actually complete despite the ticket being closed.

Swift job #SR-2026-04-1538. Greg is sending scanned PDFs; awaiting signed originals.

Discovery: original PHMSA letter (Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187, deadline May 21), Marcus Thorne's response identifying 4 shipments and requesting 15-day extension, DOT extension letter granting new deadline June 5 requiring signed originals or certified copies, and Marcus's internal forward to Chloe flagging that scanned PDFs may not suffice.

Per DOT's extension letter, signed originals or certified copies are required scans alone may not suffice.
OE 7: Search Slack #operations (C006) for hazmat/compliance discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "hazmat" or "DOT" or "Raj Patel chain" or similar) or conversations_history (channel_id: C006). Discovery: Chloe's reconstructed timeline of the Raj Patel chain-of-custody failure (SentinelChain crew loaded at 7:00 AM before the 9:00 AM escort, overnight storage at South San Francisco warehouse violated all three of Victor Huang's non-negotiables), a follow-up flagging the ticket as Done with no custody log and Airtable still showing In Progress, and Blessing Okafor's field note about Swift's driver spending 45 minutes on the DOT packet for Emilia Cruz's household move.
OE 8: Look up coordinator and recipient contact information using contacts_search_contacts or contacts_get_contacts for Javier Morales (javier.morales@moveops.com), Blessing Okafor (blessing.okafor@moveops.com), Fatimah Al-Rashidi (fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com), and Marcus Thorne (marcus.thorne@moveops.com).

Title related to "Raj Patel chain-of-custody gap" or similar, describing that the ticket was closed prematurely with no signed custody log on file, priority urgent/P1 (DOT deadline June 5), assignee Blessing Okafor or Chloe Vance.
OE 10: Create a Linear ticket or update the existing issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert (or add a comment) noting that DOT requires signed originals or certified copies per the extension letter scanned PDFs from Swift alone will not suffice for the Keiko Tanaka placarding certificate.
OE 11: Contact coordinators responsible for moves with documentation gaps via send_email or conversations_add_message Blessing Okafor (blessing.okafor@moveops.com) regarding the Raj Patel missing chain-of-custody log, and Javier Morales (javier.morales@moveops.com) regarding the Keiko Tanaka placarding certificate (need signed originals from Swift, not just scans). Both messages should reference the June 5 DOT deadline.
OE 12: Update Airtable relocation records with DOT compliance status by appending notes to the "Special Requirements" field (multilineText) using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01) recMeiLinZhaoHoustonPortland (DOT docs complete), recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle (DOT docs pending awaiting signed placarding cert from Swift), recRajPatelChicagoDenver (DOT docs gap missing signed chain-of-custody log), recJoaquinReyesPhoenixSeattle (DOT docs complete non-hazmat, oversize permit verified).

Email should cover all 4 shipments and their status, the two gaps (Tanaka placarding cert, Patel custody log), the June 5 deadline (Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187), that DOT requires signed originals, actions taken (tickets, coordinators contacted, Airtable updated), and the Raj Patel chain-of-custody failure context.
OE 14: Post a summary in Slack #operations (channel_id: C006) using conversations_add_message covering the 4 shipments' compliance status, what is complete (Zhao, Reyes), what needs action (Tanaka Javier to get signed originals from Swift; Patel Blessing to address missing custody log), the June 5 deadline, and that all documentation must be signed originals or certified copies.
📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 A Linear issue was created (via linear_create_issue) with a title related to the Raj Patel / NorthWind Technologies chain-of-custody documentation gap (or similar).
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1No Linear ticket was created for a Raj Patel / NorthWind chain-of-custody documentation gap. The agent treated Raj Patel as fully verified (concluding 'All 4 DOT doc categories complete') and created no ticket for a missing custody log. The agent read the existing Linear issue (issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind, state_done) and accepted its Done status without discovering the underlying gap.
✗Run #2No Linear ticket was created for the Raj Patel / NorthWind chain-of-custody documentation gap. The agent marked Raj Patel as 'Complete' and praised the chain-of-custody documentation as the best they'd ever seen, missing the audit note (recRajPatelAuditNote0411) showing the IT escort was bypassed and the Linear comment flagging the missing signed log. The three tickets created were OPS-483 (training), OPS-484 (Webb cryogenic), and OPS-485 (Tanaka cert).
✗Run #3No Linear ticket was created for the Raj Patel / NorthWind chain-of-custody gap. The agent trusted the 'state_done' status of issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind without reading comments, concluded Raj Patel was complete, and never opened a ticket for the missing chain-of-custody log.
✗Run #4No linear_create_issue call was made for the Raj Patel / NorthWind chain-of-custody gap. The agent accepted the existing Linear issue (issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind) at face value — it was marked state_done with 'All four documentation categories complete' — and updated the Airtable record as 'COMPLETE'. The agent never identified or flagged the missing chain-of-custody log and created no new ticket for it.
✗Run #5No Linear ticket was created for the Raj Patel / NorthWind chain-of-custody documentation gap. The agent accepted the existing 'state_done' Linear ticket (issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind) at face value without reading comments, and therefore concluded the Raj Patel move was COMPLETE. No new issue was opened for the missing custody log.
✗Run #6No Linear issue was created for the Raj Patel / NorthWind chain-of-custody gap. The agent only created two Linear tickets: OPS-483 (Keiko Tanaka placarding cert) and OPS-484 (training records). The agent mistakenly marked the Raj Patel move as COMPLETE, missing the chain-of-custody documentation gap entirely.
2 A Linear issue was created or updated, or a comment was added (via linear_create_issue, linear_update_issue, or linear_create_comment) for the Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics placarding certificate gap (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent created a new Linear issue titled 'OVERDUE — Follow up with Swift on Keiko Tanaka placarding certificate (promised April 20, never received)' via linear_create_issue. The tool response succeeded (OPS-484). This clearly addresses the Keiko Tanaka placarding certificate gap.
✓Run #2A Linear issue was created for the Keiko Tanaka placarding certificate gap (OPS-485, titled 'URGENT — Follow up on Keiko Tanaka placarding cert from Swift'). The linear_create_issue tool call was successful and returned a result.
✓Run #3The agent created a new Linear issue titled 'URGENT: Follow up with Swift on overdue Keiko Tanaka placarding certificate' (OPS-483) via linear_create_issue, which successfully addressed the Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics placarding certificate gap.
✓Run #4The agent called linear_update_issue for both issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic and issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert, updating them with current status about the pending placarding certificate. Both calls succeeded (output files returned).
✓Run #5The agent created a new Linear issue titled 'DOT Docs — Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic — Confirm Receipt of Swift Placarding Certificate' via linear_create_issue, referencing the placarding certificate gap. This passes the criterion.
✓Run #6A Linear issue was created via linear_create_issue for the Keiko Tanaka placarding certificate gap (OPS-483, issue_b5275b7f2ddf4b009aa8429d9226c39f), titled 'URGENT: Follow up with Swift on Keiko Tanaka signed placarding certificate — 4 days overdue'. The tool response confirms success.
3 The Linear ticket or comment for the Keiko Tanaka placarding gap notes that DOT requires signed originals or certified copies of the placarding certificate not just scanned PDFs (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✗✗✓✓ 67%
✓Run #1The OPS-484 ticket description explicitly states: 'The DOT requires "signed originals or certified copies" per the extension letter' and instructs to 'Request BOTH scanned PDFs (immediate) AND certified hard copies via overnight courier.' This clearly captures the signed originals/certified copies requirement.
✓Run #2The OPS-485 ticket description explicitly states: 'Request certified hard copies for the formal DOT submission via overnight courier' and the email to Greg Pallone (which the ticket references) explicitly states 'DOT requires signed originals or certified copies for the formal submission.' The ticket itself includes this requirement in its action items.
✗Run #3The Keiko Tanaka Linear ticket (OPS-483) says to 'Request certified hard copies via overnight courier in addition to scanned PDFs' but does not explicitly state that scanned PDFs alone are insufficient per the DOT extension letter requirements. It treats certified copies as additive rather than required in place of scanned PDFs.
✗Run #4Neither the update to issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic nor issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert mentions that DOT requires signed originals or certified copies and that scanned PDFs are insufficient. The update to issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert actually references Greg Pallone's offer of 'signed PDFs' without noting this format requirement is inadequate per the DOT extension letter.
✓Run #5The Keiko Tanaka Linear ticket explicitly states 'Linda Castellano's letter requires signed originals or certified copies' and asks to 'Request certified hard copies for the formal DOT submission,' satisfying the requirement that the ticket note certified copies are needed (not just scanned PDFs).
✓Run #6The Keiko Tanaka Linear ticket (OPS-483) explicitly states 'Request certified hard copies via overnight courier for the formal DOT submission,' indicating that scanned PDFs are insufficient and certified hard copies are required for DOT compliance.
4 A message was sent (via send_email or conversations_add_message) to Blessing Okafor (blessing.okafor@moveops.com).
outcome
 ✓——✗✓✗ 50%
✓Run #1A send_email tool call was made with blessing.okafor@moveops.com as one of the recipients (group email to Blessing, Fatimah, and Javier). The tool returned success with an email_id.
—Run #2No justification
—Run #3No justification
✗Run #4No send_email call was made to blessing.okafor@moveops.com and no conversations_add_message was sent directly to Blessing Okafor as an individual. The #operations post mentions @Blessing Okafor in a broadcast channel message, but no direct individual communication was sent to her.

25 The email to Marcus Thorne (via send_email) identifies that DOT requires signed originals or certified copies of the placarding certificate per the extension letter, scanned PDFs from Swift alone will not suffice (or similar)
outcome
 ✓✓✗✗✓✗ 50%
✓Run #1The email to Marcus explicitly states in the DOT requirements section: 'All must be signed originals or certified copies.' For the Tanaka gap, it notes Greg offered scanned PDFs but the overall requirements make clear scanned PDFs alone are insufficient. This captures the distinction.
✓Run #2The email to Marcus includes action item #5: 'Format complete submission package per DOT's requirements — signed originals or certified copies, organized by shipment.' And the Greg Pallone email (cc'd to Marcus) explicitly states 'DOT requires signed originals or certified copies for the formal submission.' The requirement for signed originals/certified copies (not just scanned PDFs) is communicated.
✗Run #3The email to Marcus mentions requesting 'certified hard copies via overnight courier' for the Tanaka cert but never explicitly states that scanned PDFs from Swift are insufficient per the DOT extension letter's requirement for signed originals or certified copies. The distinction is not clearly communicated.
✗Run #4The email to Marcus mentions Greg Pallone 'committed to sending scanned PDFs within 24 hours' but does not mention that the DOT extension letter requires signed originals or certified copies, nor that scanned PDFs alone are insufficient. This critical format distinction is missing from the Marcus email.
✓Run #5The email to Marcus explicitly states 'Linda Castellano at PHMSA has specified that all documents must be signed originals or certified copies' (in the Greg email, which is CC'd to Marcus), and the Marcus summary email repeatedly references needing 'certified copies' and 'certified hard copies' from Swift, implying scanned PDFs alone are insufficient.
✗Run #6The Marcus email describes the Keiko Tanaka placarding gap as 'Greg Pallone promised scanned PDFs within 24hrs... NOT RECEIVED' but does not state that scanned PDFs are insufficient for DOT compliance and that signed originals or certified copies are required. The distinction between PDF scans and certified copies is only mentioned in the email to Greg Pallone, not in the Marcus summary.

tool selection

29 The model must pass a query or filter related to 'hazmat,' 'DOT,' 'Class 9,' 'lithium,' 'laser,' 'special requirements,' or similar when identifying DOT-relevant relocation records in Airtable either via airtable_search_records parameters or by reviewing airtable_list_records results and filtering for hazmat/DOT-related content (or similar).
query construction
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent retrieved all records via airtable_list_records and then reviewed them to identify records with hazmat/DOT-related Special Requirements fields (Class 9 lithium, Class 3B laser, chain-of-custody, DOT certification requirements). The criterion allows for reviewing list results and filtering for hazmat content, which the agent did.
✓Run #2The agent called airtable_list_records and retrieved all records, then reviewed the results identifying records with DOT/hazmat-related special requirements (HAZMAT flags, Class 9 lithium, Class 3B laser, liquid nitrogen). The agent correctly identified the 5 DOT-relevant moves from reviewing the full record set.
✓Run #3The agent used airtable_list_records to retrieve all relocation records and then reviewed the Special Requirements field content to identify hazmat-classified moves (Class 9 lithium batteries, Class 3B laser, etc.), effectively filtering for DOT/hazmat-relevant records from the results.
✓Run #4The agent called airtable_list_records to retrieve all relocation records, then reviewed the results and identified records with hazmat/DOT-related special requirements (HAZMAT mentions, Class 9, Class 3B laser, DOT references). The 'or' condition of the criterion is satisfied — the agent reviewed list results and filtered for hazmat/DOT content.
✓Run #5The agent used airtable_list_records to retrieve all records, then reviewed the results and identified records with DOT/hazmat-related special requirements (e.g., records mentioning 'Class 9 lithium-ion,' 'Class 3B CO2 laser,' 'hazmat,' 'Class 2.2 liquid nitrogen'). This qualifies as reviewing list results and filtering for hazmat content.
✓Run #6The agent called airtable_list_records to retrieve all records and then reviewed the results, identifying hazmat-related records based on the 'Special Requirements' field content referencing Class 9, hazmat, DOT, and laser classifications.
